items.title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
items.summary: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.

items.title: Best Kitchen Composters and Food Recyclers (2026)

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook Is Stepping Down
items.summary: John Ternus, the company’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will replace Cook as CEO on September 1. Cook will stay on as executive chairman.

items.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in seven new countries
items.summary: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam.

items.title: Tim Cook stepping down as Apple CEO, John Ternus taking over
items.summary: The transition has been expected for some time and ends one of the longer and more impactful runs a CEO has had at any company. Cook had inherited a company that many industry watchers and enthusiasts struggled to separate from its famed founder. What he leaves behind is a $4 trillion business with annual revenue that has more than quadrupled on his watch.

items.title: Google Photos adds new touch-up tools for ‘quick’ fixes
items.summary: You can use the new tools to remove blemishes, refine skin texture, brighten eyes, or whiten teeth in photos.

items.title: Outrage over Israeli soldier's vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
items.summary: The Israeli military says it views with "great severity" the actions of the soldier, as the US ambassador demands "swift" consequences.

items.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
items.summary: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

items.summary: In a 2026 market that had Microsoft down close to 20%, looking for opportunities to buy after big drawdowns is coming to more tech and software stocks.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: After 200 years scientists finally crack the “dolomite problem”
items.summary: After two centuries of failed attempts, scientists have finally grown dolomite in the lab, cracking a long-standing geological puzzle. They discovered that the mineral’s growth stalls because of tiny defects—but in nature, those flaws get washed away over time. By mimicking this process with precise simulations and electron beam pulses, the team achieved record-breaking crystal growth. The finding could reshape how high-tech materials are made.

items.title: A new force of nature is reshaping the planet, study finds
items.summary: Human societies didn’t just adapt to the planet—they learned to reshape it. From early fire use to today’s global supply chains, our cultural and social innovations have unlocked extraordinary power to transform Earth and improve human life. But that progress has come with serious costs, including climate change, pollution, and mass extinction. Instead of framing this era—the Anthropocene—as pure crisis, Erle Ellis argues it’s also proof of something hopeful: when people work together, they can drive massive positive change.

items.title: Scientists develop dirt-powered fuel cell that could replace batteries
items.summary: Scientists have developed a fuel cell that uses microbes in soil to produce electricity. The device can power underground sensors for tasks like monitoring moisture or detecting touch, without needing batteries or solar panels. It works in both dry and wet conditions and even lasts longer than similar technologies. This could pave the way for sustainable, low-maintenance sensors in farming and environmental monitoring.

items.title: Increasing heat can boost malnutrition among children
items.summary: In a study of 6.5 million children in Brazil, higher temperatures were associated with worse nutrition outcomes, especially in vulnerable groups.

items.title: This kea parrot is the first-known disabled alpha male
items.summary: With half a beak, Bruce has developed an innovative fighting style that has won the kea top status in his flock, videos and documented interactions reveal.

items.title: A vaccine for Lyme disease could be on the horizon
items.summary: The vaccine candidate is the furthest any shot has gotten since the last one was pulled in 2002. Scientists are testing other ways to block infection.
